parsed_review.summary: CP00-271 is a clean descriptor-only closeout pack that closes the RP07 descriptor scope (P00–P09) and routes to the RP08 program bootstrap at CP00-272 (RP08.P00.M00.S01). All pack-level claims are supported by read-only inspection of source: the CP271 pack binding (registry.js:649), requirements (registry.js:3214), and no-write attestation (registry.js:3207) match the baseline exactly (risk C, 86 units, range RP07.P09.M06.S07–RP07.P09.M10.S10, deliverable distribution impl 57/claude_review 12/security_audit 8/test 4/ui 4/hermes_evidence 1, micro-title counts 14/22/20/20/10). All 7 required public exports exist across service.js/validators.js and are re-exported via index.js `export *`. The no-write/no-real-data boundary holds: freezeCp271Result (service.js:4826) and the attestation set every runtime/write/storage/audit/Hermes-receipt flag false, omit unauthorized data, exclude raw payloads, and set closes_rp07_descriptor_scope=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. Per-row guards (descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only, raw_payload_included=false) plus permission-bypass/UI-leak/Claude-approval/human-approval/command-rerun guards propagate through an intact CP247→CP268 row-extras chain and are enforced by validateSearchCoreCp271P09CloseoutDescriptor. Claude is held as a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false; allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob), and Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. The pack does not overclaim: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready=false (descriptor-verification flag only), with enterprise trust deferred to human approval outside the receipt. Plan continuity is verified (RP07.P09.M10 ends at S10; CP00-272 begins RP08.P00.M00.S01, no orphaned units). The contract projection deep-equals the source registry in the validation script, and tests assert coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, RP07 closure, and RP08 routing. Review-evidence hardening checks pass (auth preflight succeeded with valid non-empty JSON; prompt 2296B within limit; no full diff/source embedded; tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob; schema present). No P0/P1/P2 issues; one cosmetic P3 in generated review-harness metadata, non-blocking.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-classifies the pack's own manifest directory as both implementation and unrelated dirty file
findings.details: docs/closeout-packs/cp00-271/ appears in untracked_implementation_files (line 97) and is simultaneously listed in unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (line 103). That directory holds CP00-271's own manifest.json and command-evidence.json, so tagging it 'unrelated' is mildly inconsistent. This is harmless for review correctness: active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty, so no required deliverable was dropped from scope, and the active_pack_implementation_refs list independently captures the eight tracked implementation files. This is generated review-harness bookkeeping metadata, not a CP00-271 product deliverable.
findings.recommendation: In the changed-file-scope generator, exclude the pack's own docs/closeout-packs/<pack-id>/ directory from unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (or stop also listing it under untracked_implementation_files) so the classification is unambiguous. Non-blocking.
findings.post_review_disposition: Informational only; does not affect review scope, boundary attestations, or pack correctness. No change required before closeout.
